data.title: Regions Financial Earnings Are Imminent; These Most Accurate Analysts Revise Forecasts Ahead Of Earnings Call
data.description: Regions Financial Corporation (NYSE: RF) is set to unveil its first-quarter earnings on April 17. Analysts anticipate the Birmingham-based company to announce earnings of 59 cents per share, a rise from the previous year's 54 cents per share. The consensus estimate for the quarter's revenue stands at $1.92 billion, an increase from the previous year's $1.8 billion as per Benzinga Pro. Also, on April 15, the company declared dividends for both common and preferred stocks. The company's shares closed at $27.92 on Thursday, up by 0.3%. For further insights, readers can explore the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga to view the most-accurate analysts' ratings for the company.

data.title: Top Wall Street Forecasters Revamp Ally Financial Expectations Ahead Of Q1 Earnings
data.description: Ally Financial Inc. is set to announce first-quarter earnings on April 17 before the market opens. Analysts are predicting earnings per share of 94 cents, an increase from 58 cents in the same period last year. Revenue for the quarter is estimated to be around $2.14 billion, up from $1.54 billion in the previous year. This financial information comes from Benzinga Pro. Earlier this week, the company's board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of 30 cents per share for common stockholders. The stock price of Ally Financial closed at $41.96 on Thursday, showing a 0.7% decrease. For more details and analyst ratings, readers can visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on the Benzinga website.

data.title: Truist Financial Gears Up For Q1 Print; Here Are The Recent Forecast Changes From Wall Street's Most Accurate Analysts
data.description: Truist Financial Corporation (NYSE: TFC) is set to announce its first-quarter earnings report on Friday, April 17. Analysts are anticipating the Charlotte-based company to reveal earnings of $1.00 per share, an increase from 89 cents per share in the same quarter last year. The projected revenue for this quarter stands at $5.18 billion, compared to $4.95 billion reported last year. Truist Securities appointed Matthew Miller as the head of mergers & acquisitions on Feb. 26. The company's shares closed at $49.43 on Thursday, seeing a 0.3% drop. For further insights, readers can check out Benzinga's latest analyst ratings on their Analyst Stock Ratings page. Visit Benzinga.com for the complete story.

data.title: Ethereum researcher Josh Stark departs Ethereum Foundation, the latest prominent exit since the Q1 2025 reorganization.
data.description: Stark's exit from the Ethereum Foundation marks a significant departure from the organization following its restructuring in early 2025. This move is considered the most notable exit since the reorganization, reflecting important changes within the foundation.

data.title: European Firms Deploy GenAI in Mainframe Modernization
data.description: In London, European companies are increasingly integrating mainframe systems and transitioning from experimental to operational use of GenAI within standardized workflows, according to ISG. This shift signifies a significant advancement in how businesses are adopting artificial intelligence technology. The move suggests that companies are embracing AI more methodically and incorporating it into established processes. This trend reflects the changing landscape of technology integration in the business sector.
